title: Lamb Tikka Masala

Main: 1 1/2 pounds lamb leg sirloin, trimmed and cut into 1-inch cubes
1 medium serrano chile, seeded and minced
Garam Masala: 1 dried arbol chile, stemmed, seeded and crumbled

steps: Combine 1 tablespoon of the garam masala, 1 teaspoon of the salt, cumin, coriander, and pepper in a 1-gallon resealable bag. Add the lamb and shake to coat. Add the yogurt, seal, and squish to coat. Refrigerate in a leak-proof container for 30 minutes and up to 1 hour.
Spritz 4 to 5 pieces of newspaper with vegetable oil and place in the bottom of a charcoal chimney starter. Fill the chimney with half of the charcoal and light the newspaper.

When the charcoal is lightly covered with gray ash, carefully pour it onto the bottom grate of a kettle grill and spread evenly. Top with the remaining unlit charcoal. Set the second grate in the kettle and cover until ready to cook.

Heat the vegetable oil in a 12-inch straight-sided saute pan over medium-high until it shimmers and almost begins to smoke. Add the onion and remaining teaspoon of salt; cook until browned around the edges, 11 to 12 minutes, stirring occasionally.

Reduce heat to medium-low and add the garlic, ginger, and chile. Cook, stirring constantly, until the onions have softened and browned completely, about 7 minutes. Sprinkle with the remaining tablespoon of garam masala and stir several times to coat.
Add the tomatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, until they have reduced slightly and deepened in color, 15 to 20 minutes.

Remove the lamb from the yogurt mixture, leaving as much yogurt on the meat as possible. Thread the pieces of lamb, 1/4-inch apart, onto metal skewers. Grill until the yogurt is slightly charred or blackened, about 2 minutes on each side.

Remove the lamb from the skewers. Add the lamb and coconut milk to the tomato mixture; stir to combine and heat through. Transfer to a serving bowl, garnish with mint or cilantro, and serve over basmati rice.

To make the garam masala: combine all of the seeds, peppercorns, cloves, chile, and cinnamon in an 8-inch cast iron skillet over medium-high heat. Cook, moving the pan constantly, until you smell the cumin toasting, about 3 to 4 minutes. Remove to a plate and cool for 5 minutes.
Add the toasted mixture and nutmeg to a spice grinder. Process until a fine powder forms, about 1 minute. Use immediately or store in an airtight container for up to 1 month.
